best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest contributor: 31 trades, $2419.4 PnL, $252.9/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. Worst MAE is meaningful at -403.2 but not the portfolio tail.

best_edges.edge: 1DTE fallback breakout edge when 0DTE is unavailable
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte contributes 20 trades and $1315.3 in VALID_1DTE_ONLY state with no slow trades. It is a stable data-state edge, although efficiency is lower at $170.1/hour and avg MAE is -124.6.

charm_pin_pullback_long contributes 29 trades and $1077.4 with median hold 3.0 minutes, $208.9/hour, avg MAE only -57.8, and worst MAE -335.75. It overlaps extended-above-VWAP winners, so broad VWAP caps are dangerous.

charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 5 trades but $987.0 PnL, $234.7/hour, median TP 12, no slow trades, and worst MAE -357. It has repeatedly improved prior branches when structure-confirmed admission was preserved.

call_wall_acceptance_long has the best active VALID_0DTE efficiency at $290.3/hour and $640.5 from 2 trades, but it also owns the portfolio worst MAE -655.2. It is real but too small and tail-heavy for broad promotion.
squeeze_long adds 10 trades, $723.0 PnL, no slow trades, and the cleanest major-profile worst MAE at -214.2. It should be preserved as an option-map/order-book confirmed continuation slice.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE produced only 5 trades and $162.8 total. breakout_long_no_0dte stale slice has 2 trades, $71.0, $40.5/hour, median hold 52.6 minutes, and avg MAE -210.6.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE fallback is positive but low efficiency and sample-fragile; stale breakout fallback is especially capital-inefficient.

broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long are active in router lists but disabled or produced zero trades in this run.
broken_edges.problem: Dead or unreachable enabled-router concepts should not receive parameter optimization until activated as isolated attribution tests.

broken_edges.evidence: Worst tail was call_wall_acceptance_long: $546 PnL, -655.2 MAE, 120.4 minute hold. Broad setup floor would retain only 19.1% PnL, and broad hidden signatures retain only 70-83% PnL.
broken_edges.problem: The largest tail is also a major winner, so blunt pruning destroys too much edge. Needs split/containment research, not global gating.

broken_edges.evidence: Extended_vwap_winners have 18 matches and $1051.9 PnL with median MAE -17.85, while bad-MAE near misses are covered by the same extended-VWAP rejection family.
broken_edges.problem: The same extension state creates both clean winners and bad MAE. Broad ES-VWAP max-distance tightening risks blocking protected winners.

market_regime_read: Short 12.3-day, high-win-rate, take-profit-only long scalper regime. The edge is broad across VALID_0DTE pullback/charm/squeeze, VALID_1DTE fallback, and a small convex call-wall sample. Realized losses and slow >=12h trades are absent, so the risk-adjusted bottleneck is hidden MAE and long recovery time, especially call-wall acceptance and a few pullback/1DTE heat rows. Because protected fast winners overlap the bad-MAE signatures, the next work should discover profile/data-state splits rather than apply broad quality filters.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separates 1DTE pullback/reclaim states from clean 1DTE breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Cannibalizes profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or is unreachable under current router logic.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY option map, ES reclaim/pullback/structure state, existing 1DTE breakout attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state looks like pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1315, 1DTE average/worst MAE flat or better than -124.6/-386.75, breakout_long_1dte retained PnL explicitly measured, total score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Quarantines convex call-wall trades that show early progress but delayed realization and deep heat.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Overfits a two-trade profile and cuts the largest winner.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call-Wall Convex Recovery Monitor
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path progress, half-target timing, MAE-before-TP sequencing, target distance, call-wall profile attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: call_wall_acceptance_long enters with wide target or extended ES-VWAP and fails to convert early favorable movement into sustained continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves below -655.2 toward <= -513.4 while retaining >=70% call-wall PnL, >=98% total PnL, no slow trades, and protected fast-winner retention >=95%.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Detects pullback/charm-pin entries that do not make early positive progress before adverse expansion.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Can block delayed but ultimately profitable pullback/charm-pin recoveries.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Early Progress Heat Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: First-profit timing, first 15m MFE/MAE, MFE-before-MAE flag, profile attribution, protected winner UTC holdout.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: pullback_long or charm_pin_pullback_long entry with no meaningful early progress before MAE expansion.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce affected-profile worst MAE while retaining >=90% pullback/charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast winners, total score flat or better, slow_12h remains 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Tests whether dormant pin-avoid logic can absorb high-extension charm/pin tails without broad filters.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant route may be unreachable or may cannibalize cleaner charm_wall_breakaway_long/charm_pin_pullback_long trades.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Attribution Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Valid-0DTE router attribution, dormant profile standalone PnL, affected-profile cannibalization, protected fast-winner retention.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm-wall/charm-pin context near pin-risk or wall-breakaway failure zones.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile positive standalone PnL or lower combined charm/pin worst MAE, total PnL >=98% of leader, protected fast-winner retention >=95%, no slow trades.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Explores whether delta/charm breakout is a separate high-quality continuation regime from squeeze and charm-wall breakaway.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May duplicate squeeze/charm-wall selection and reduce their PnL without adding net edge.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Delta Charm Breakout Dormant Attribution Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE router replacement, standalone dormant profile PnL, squeeze/charm-wall cannibalization, MAE comparison.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE strong option-flow/charm continuation state that is not cleanly captured by squeeze_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Positive standalone PnL, no more than 10% degradation in squeeze/charm-wall combined PnL, total PnL >=98% of leader, worst MAE no worse than -513.4.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use a full valid-0DTE router attribution test for exactly one dormant pin-avoid concept plus minimal same-profile tuning. Do not activate multiple dormant profiles together.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether an existing dormant pin-avoid concept can split tail-heavy charm/pin/wall states from profitable continuation states.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Some bad-MAE rows are not poor global entries; they are misrouted pin/wall acceptance states that need a separate profile label.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Attribution Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Standalone dormant profile PnL, combined charm/pin/wall worst MAE, total risk-adjusted score, protected fast-winner retention.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample and router cannibalization; reject if total PnL drops below 98% or protected fast winners are blocked.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte baseline stable. Test companion routing only if legal, and measure cannibalization explicitly rather than tightening the current 1DTE winner.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether VALID_1DTE_ONLY is two regimes: breakout continuation and pullback/reclaim fallback.
next_experiments.hypothesis: A separate 1DTE pullback companion can improve 1DTE MAE/efficiency without damaging the $1315 breakout_long_1dte edge.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Split Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, breakout_long_1dte retained trades/PnL, total score.
next_experiments.risk: Companion may be unreachable or may steal clean breakout_long_1dte winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Isolate call_wall_acceptance_long only. Favor persistence/realization/sample-split research over global call-volume or setup-quality pruning.
next_experiments.goal: Separate call-wall convex winners from long-heat recovery paths while keeping the profile alive.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The call-wall edge is real but needs a distinct containment box; disabling or broad tightening loses too much PnL.
next_experiments.name: Call-Wall Convex Tail Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, call_wall_acceptance_long PnL retention >=70%, total PnL retention >=98%, total score.
next_experiments.risk: Only 2 trades; any result can be overfit. Largest tail is also largest winner.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use narrow near-miss research around the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection family. Avoid broad ES-VWAP max-distance, setup-quality, RSI, or structure-score floors.
next_experiments.goal: Validate whether bad-MAE near misses can be quarantined without deleting extended-above-VWAP winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The existing veto family missed deep-MAE rows by small boundaries, but protected extended-VWAP winners require profile-aware rather than global treatment.
next_experiments.name: Extended-VWAP Near-Miss Boundary Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, retained total PnL >=98%, protected extended_vwap_winner retention >=95%, PnL/exposure flat or better.
next_experiments.risk: Near-miss rows include profitable trades; over-widening veto boundaries can suppress edge without improving kept worst MAE.

next_experiments.config_bias: Treat STALE_0DTE separately from EXPIRED_0DTE if expressible. Do not disturb VALID_0DTE routing and do not remove the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner unless state targeting is explicit.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether stale/no-0DTE fallback deserves a stricter or separate realization profile.
next_experiments.hypothesis: No-0DTE fallback is positive but less efficient; stale breakout fallback may be a capital drag that needs separate treatment from expired 0DTE continuation.
next_experiments.name: No-0DTE Data-State Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/hour, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL/trade count, total PnL retention >=98%.
next_experiments.risk: Very small sample; broad fallback changes can remove the one high-efficiency EXPIRED_0DTE winner.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep option-map and order-book confirmation strict. Study squeeze as a clean continuation benchmark, not as a place to loosen admission.
next_experiments.goal: Confirm whether squeeze_long can remain a clean, low-tail continuation contributor while other profiles are split.
next_experiments.hypothesis: squeeze_long has the cleanest major-profile worst MAE and can serve as a protected continuation edge during new-regime discovery.
next_experiments.name: Squeeze Clean Continuation Benchmark
next_experiments.primary_metric: squeeze_long PnL, squeeze worst MAE no worse than -214.2 to -250 tolerance, total net profit >=98%, total score flat or better.
next_experiments.risk: Exit or target experiments may reduce total PnL if rare larger continuation moves are underpaid.

research_notes: Do not emit direct config changes from this plan. The config optimizer should translate these into legal config-only experiments. The current leader family is strong on raw score and PnL but weaker than robust-score leaders on drawdown/worst MAE: run_055 has $7532.8 PnL and -655.2 worst MAE, while robust leaders have about $7149.6 PnL and -513.4 worst MAE. New-regime work should prioritize attribution: router/data-state splits, dormant concept probes, and path-aware ideas for later code work. Broad single-threshold filters are unattractive because protected fast winners and extended-VWAP winners overlap the bad-MAE state.
risk_controls: Require total net profit retention >=98% of run_055 unless the experiment explicitly targets robust-score improvement and materially improves worst MAE.
Reject experiments that reduce completed trades by more than 10% without a clear score and MAE improvement.
Keep slow_12h trades at 0; any reintroduction of slow trades is a hard negative.
Track protected fast-winner and extended_vwap_winner retention; do not accept broad filters that delete these states.
Measure cannibalization explicitly for dormant and companion router tests: standalone new-profile PnL is not enough.
Do not optimize dead active profiles unless the experiment is an isolated activation/attribution test.
